author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Analysis: binary_map() uses per-element flat index decomposition with ndim modulo+division ops per element. No SIMD for broadcast. Same-shape ops are fast (ferray SIMD) but broadcast falls off a cliff.

Plan: Replace binary_map's index decomposition loop with stride-based iteration. The key insight: when broadcasting, one or more input dimensions have stride 0. We can iterate using precomputed strides directly instead of decomposing flat indices. For the common case (broadcast along leading/trailing dims), we can emit tight inner loops that are SIMD-friendly.

Approach:
A. Rewrite binary_map to use stride-based iteration with an ndim counter array (no modulo/division)
B. Special-case the innermost contiguous dimension for SIMD  
C. Add rayon parallelism for large broadcast ops
